output-exists.note: framework bug: testPattern matches describe block name but JUnit parser only extracts testcase names
framework bug: testPattern 'reconciliation\.json exists' does not match testcase name 'reconciliation.json file is present'

admission-count.note: framework bug: testPattern 'admission list has 5' does not match testcase name 'admission list has exactly 5 medications'

discharge-count.note: framework bug: testPattern 'discharge list has 6' does not match testcase name 'discharge list has exactly 6 medications'

changes_made: Compressed verbose Python code examples — model generates its own implementation
Made output JSON schemas ultra-explicit with exact field names (admission_meds, discharge_meds, changes)
Added concrete JSON examples for each medication reconciliation change category
Emphasized medication count accuracy and negation scope rules
Removed redundant ICD-10 code references and duplicate drug list entries
framework_bugs_found: testPattern matching uses JUnit <testcase name=...> but patterns were designed for <testsuite name=...> (describe block names). 4 of 12 criteria are impossible to pass: output-exists on both tasks, admission-count, and discharge-count.
Pattern 'entities\.json exists' cannot match testcase names 'entities.json file is present' or 'entities.json is a non-empty array'
Pattern 'admission list has 5' cannot match 'admission list has exactly 5 medications' (word 'exactly' breaks substring match)
consistency: 3 consecutive runs all produced identical scores (90 + 60), confirming model produces correct output reliably
